text:
Matthew Ebden reacted with a look of horror after being handed the most difficult draw imaginable ahead of the men's singles tennis tournament at the2024 Olympics.

Ebden is only participating after stepping in at the last minute as an alternate, having been selected to represent Australia in the doubles' draw.

The 36-year-old has been brought into the singles' drawfollowing the withdrawal of Jannik Sinnerand Hubert Hurkacz.Andy Murray then pulled out hours beforethe draw, leaving an unassigned slot in the draw to face Sebastian Ofner.

Our community members are treated to special offers, promotions, and adverts from us and our partners. You can check out at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

The Australian got his reward from being included in the draw as he was pittedagainst 24-time Grand Slam winner Novak Djokovicin the opening round of the knockout tournament.

As if that wasn't tough enough, Ebden could then face Spain'sRafael Nadalin the next round - and that's only if he were to beat Djokovic against the odds.

Needless to say, the veteran player was under no illusions about the task facing him in Paris. Reacting to the news on social media, Ebden posted a video to his Instagram account with a hilarious expression painted across his face.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

The veteran star jokingly stared into the camera looking aghast and his eyes then widened during the brief four-second clip.

The caption on the video read: "When you find out you got into alternate into the Olympics singles only to draw @djokernole 1st round to play @rafaelnadal in the 2nd round."

And it's fair to say Ebden didn't rate his chances of claiming a shock win for his country either, picking the 'Mission Impossible' theme tune to play in the background to sum up his thoughts about the draw.

Matt Ebden’s reaction to finding out he plays Novak Djokovic in the 1st round & Rafa Nadal in the 2nd round of the Olympicspic.twitter.com/oAXhLb5qKm

Ebden's career in tennis has mainly centred around his journey to becoming world No. 1 in the men's doubles. He reached the top of theATPrankings in February this year and is currently ranked third, with two doubles' Majors to his name atWimbledonin 2022 and theAustralian Openin 2024.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

His career-best singles' ranking saw him rise to 39th in the world in 2018, although he has never progressed beyond the third round of a Major, which he achieved in Wimbledon six years ago. As a contrast, Djokovic and Nadal are two of the most decorated tennis players of all time and have 46 Grand Slam titles between them.

Of course, the Olympics provides a different setting to ATP Tour tennis, with Djokovic only ever managing a bronze medal at Beijing in 2008. With that in mind, Ebden may fancy his chances on the clay courts at Roland Garros, where Djokovic has won the fewest titles out of all four Slams.

title: Olympics star leaves tennis fans in stitches reacting to Djokovic draw | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Joe Krishnan
description: One Olympics tennis star has been handed a toughest draw imaginable with the prospect of facing Novak Djokovic and potentially Rafael Nadal in Paris.
